name: Jai Allocator API
summary: Jai's implicit context carrying allocator + temp allocator, threaded through calls, with `push_allocator` (or double-comma shorthand) to rebind allocator policy for a subtree of calls without manual parameter threading.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Jai (closed beta; public descriptions are community-derived) is included as historical influence: an implicit context carrying allocator + temp allocator, threaded through calls, with `push_allocator` or double-comma shorthand to rebind allocator policy for a subtree of calls without manual parameter threading. Odin's context allocator design is publicly documented as Jai-inspired.

For the Jai language as a whole — closed-beta status, custom x64 + LLVM dual backend, compiler-as-library design — see the dedicated `jai-language` record.

sections.title: push_allocator and temporary storage
sections.content:
Within a function, `push_allocator(my_allocator) { ... }` swaps the implicit context allocator for the duration of the block; on exit the previous allocator is restored. The double-comma shorthand `foo(args,, my_allocator)` rebinds for a single call. Temporary storage (`temp` or `__temporary_storage`) is a built-in arena reset at user-controlled frame boundaries; library data structures consult it implicitly.

Status (as of 2026-04): Jai remains in closed beta; the allocator API design is documented through community wiki resources rather than first-party reference. Influence is still significant — Odin's context allocator and several other languages cite Jai as source.

sources.label: The Way to Jai: Memory Allocators and Temporary Storage
